methodology: Leave-One-Year-Out backtest. Scored v2 decision matrices (Phase 5B tree-aware) against actual wines from data/exams.json. Variety synonym matching applied (Syrah=Shiraz, Garnacha=Grenache, Spatburgunder=Pinot Noir, Cot=Malbec, Monastrell=Mourvedre, Pinot Grigio=Pinot Gris). Appellation-to-variety resolution via data/appellation_varieties.json. Blends: hit if any predicted component matches any actual component.

results.question_structure: 4 wines, 2 countries, 1 common variety (may be blends)
results.notes: Common variety Sauvignon Blanc predicted as STRONG SIGNAL top-1. All 4 origins (Loire, Marlborough, Pessac-Leognan, Marlborough) predicted exactly. W4 low-alcohol (9.5%) style not specifically anticipated but country/variety correct.

per_wine.notes: Rioja Blanco predicted exactly. Viura is the variety used in Rioja Blanco.

Matrix predicted Soave/Garganega or Sicilian white for Italian pair. Grillo was listed as an alternative (Grillo or Vermentino or Fiano) in universe. Country and Sicily correct. Variety rank 0 because Grillo was not the named top candidate (Soave/Garganega was). However Grillo was mentioned in candidate text. Scoring as top-3 hit since Grillo explicitly named in candidate list.
Moscato d'Asti predicted as PLAUSIBLE curveball within Italian pair. Country and Piedmont correct.

results.notes: 5/6 top-1 variety hits. Grillo was mentioned in candidate origins but not as the named top variety for W7 (Soave/Garganega was). Moscato d'Asti predicted as curveball (rank 2). All 6 countries and 5/6 sub-regions correct.

results.question_structure: 2 wines, 2 Old World countries, no origin ID required
results.prediction_level: variety+origin per wine (despite stem saying don't ID origin)

per_wine.notes: Georgian qvevri amber wine predicted as STRONG SIGNAL top candidate. Chinuri variety named explicitly.
Vecchio Samperi (Marco de Bartoli) named explicitly as STRONG SIGNAL candidate. Grillo variety resolved via appellation_varieties.json. Sicily predicted.

results.notes: Both wines predicted exactly including specific wine names (Chinuri/Georgian qvevri, Vecchio Samperi/Marco de Bartoli). Perfect score despite the stem directing candidates away from origin.

per_wine.notes: Bordeaux right bank Merlot-dominant predicted. Lussac-Saint-Emilion is a satellite; matrix predicted right bank (Saint-Emilion satellites or Fronsac). Close sub-region match.
Super Tuscan with Cab Sauv/Petit Verdot predicted exactly. Vigna d'Alceo named in appellation_varieties.json.
Loire Cabernet Franc (Saumur-Champigny) predicted as STRONG SIGNAL.
Stellenbosch Bordeaux blend predicted as PLAUSIBLE. Vergelegen 'V' resolved via appellation_varieties.json.

results.notes: All 4 variety families correct. All 4 countries correct. Lussac-Saint-Emilion counted as sub-region hit (satellite of Saint-Emilion, predicted as right bank).

results.question_structure: 4 wines, 4 countries, North and South America
results.notes: Perfect prediction. All 4 countries, all 4 varieties, all 4 sub-regions predicted as STRONG SIGNAL top-1.

per_wine.notes: Spatburgunder/Pinot Noir from Germany predicted as STRONG SIGNAL. Mosel as specific sub-region noted as unusual but named.
Blaufrankisch from Leithaberg/Burgenland predicted as STRONG SIGNAL.
Douro Portuguese red predicted as STRONG SIGNAL.
Villanyi Franc = Cabernet Franc from Villany. Predicted exactly including Vylyan Winery.

results.notes: Perfect prediction. All 4 countries, all 4 varieties, all sub-regions correct. The tree's second-tier European reds pattern is its most reliable.

per_wine.notes: NV Champagne predicted as STRONG SIGNAL. Champagne varieties resolved via appellation_varieties.json.
Vintage Champagne predicted. Egly-Ouriet 2007 Grand Cru is grower vintage Champagne.

results.notes: Champagne NV vs vintage predicted exactly. Region and varieties all correct.

per_wine.notes: Prosecco Superiore DOCG from Valdobbiadene predicted as STRONG SIGNAL. Glera resolved via appellation_varieties.json (Prosecco).
Basic Prosecco DOC predicted as STRONG SIGNAL.

results.notes: Prosecco DOCG vs DOC predicted exactly as top candidate.

per_wine.notes: Provence rose with Grenache/Cinsault/Mourvedre predicted as STRONG SIGNAL.
Rioja rosado predicted as STRONG SIGNAL.
California Pinot Noir rose predicted. Russian River Valley Sonoma sub-region correct.

NZ rose predicted. Matrix suggested Pinot Noir or commercial blend for NZ. Actual is Kim Crawford which is Pinot Noir/Merlot blend. Pinot Noir component predicted. Sub-region not specific (NZ-wide). Variety scored as top-3 hit because Pinot Noir was named as candidate but blend with Merlot adds complexity. Scoring Pinot Noir as partial hit (rank 2 since exact blend not predicted).

results.notes: Rose category correctly predicted. All 4 countries correct. 3/4 varieties exact top-1. W8 NZ rose predicted Pinot Noir but actual was Pinot Noir/Merlot blend - Pinot Noir component matches.

per_wine.notes: Manzanilla from Sanlucar predicted exactly. Palomino resolved via appellation_varieties.json (manzanilla entry). Lustau named.
Jura oxidative from L'Etoile predicted exactly. Cuvee Speciale, Domaine de Montbourgeau resolved via appellation_varieties.json. Chardonnay/Savagnin varieties match.

results.notes: Sherry (Manzanilla) + Jura oxidative pair predicted exactly as STRONG SIGNAL top candidate.

Rutherglen Muscat predicted as STRONG SIGNAL. Campbells named in candidate list.

results.notes: Passito (Italy/Sicily) vs Rutherglen Muscat (Australia) predicted exactly. Both specific wines anticipated.

per_wine.notes: Vin Jaune (Savagnin) from Arbois predicted as STRONG SIGNAL.
Corton Charlemagne (Chardonnay Grand Cru Burgundy) predicted as STRONG SIGNAL.
Rhone white blend from Cotes du Rhone-Villages predicted as PLAUSIBLE. Inopia Blanc resolved via appellation_varieties.json.

results.question_structure: 3 wines, same single grape variety, quality/maturity comparison

per_wine.notes: Chenin Blanc predicted as STRONG SIGNAL. Anjou entry-level Loire predicted.
Aged Savennieres Chenin predicted. 2007 vintage (14 years old at exam) exactly anticipated.
South Africa artisan Chenin predicted. Citrusdal Mountains sub-region matches prediction.

results.notes: Chenin Blanc predicted as STRONG SIGNAL (quality/maturity emphasis was the routing signal). All 3 origins predicted exactly including quality tier differentiation (entry Anjou, premium Savennieres, mid-tier SA).

per_wine.notes: Riesling Federspiel from Wachau (FX Pichler) predicted as STRONG SIGNAL. Producer and classification level exactly anticipated.
Riesling Smaragd from Wachau (FX Pichler) predicted as STRONG SIGNAL. Producer, variety, classification, and region all correct.

results.notes: FX Pichler Wachau Riesling Federspiel vs Smaragd predicted exactly. The tree's most precise single-producer prediction.

per_wine.notes: Gallo Moscato from California predicted as STRONG SIGNAL. 9% ABV was the diagnostic signal.
Patz & Hall Carneros Chardonnay predicted as STRONG SIGNAL.
Ponzi Pinot Gris from Willamette Valley Oregon predicted as PLAUSIBLE.

results.notes: Country USA predicted as STRONG SIGNAL (after France excluded from Q1). All 3 varieties and sub-regions correct. Ponzi named explicitly in candidates.

per_wine.notes: Bordeaux Saint-Julien predicted as STRONG SIGNAL. Ducru-Beaucaillou named explicitly. 2013 difficult vintage predicted.
2015 excellent vintage predicted.
2005 legendary vintage predicted.

results.notes: Bordeaux classified growth vertical predicted exactly. Ducru-Beaucaillou named as candidate producer. Saint-Julien commune-level prediction. All 3 vintages (2013, 2015, 2005) predicted.

per_wine.notes: Pinot Noir from Sonoma predicted as STRONG SIGNAL.
Nebbiolo/Barbaresco predicted as PLAUSIBLE top European classic.
Grenache from McLaren Vale predicted as PLAUSIBLE. D'Arenberg The Custodian named explicitly.

results.notes: All 3 continents, 3 varieties, 3 sub-regions predicted correctly. D'Arenberg The Custodian named explicitly in candidate analysis.

per_wine.notes: Petite Sirah from California predicted as PLAUSIBLE (upgraded from CURVEBALL after tree confirmation). Bogle named explicitly.
Carmenere from Cachapoal Chile predicted as STRONG SIGNAL. Terrunyo Concha y Toro named explicitly.

results.notes: Both varieties and origins predicted correctly including specific producer names (Bogle, Terrunyo). Low ID marks (10 each) correctly interpreted as signal for less-obvious variety.

results.question_structure: 4 wines in 2 pairs, each pair from different region

per_wine.notes: CdP predicted as STRONG SIGNAL for Rhone pair. Clos des Papes named. Grenache-dominant confirmed via appellation_varieties.json.
CdR predicted as STRONG SIGNAL for Rhone pair. Guigal named.
Rioja Crianza predicted as STRONG SIGNAL for Rioja pair. Tempranillo-dominant.
Rioja Gran Reserva predicted as STRONG SIGNAL. Castillo Ygay Marques de Murrieta named explicitly.

results.notes: Both pairs (Rhone CdP vs CdR, Rioja Crianza vs Gran Reserva) predicted exactly as STRONG SIGNAL. All 4 wines' origins and varieties correct.

per_wine.notes: NV Champagne (Moet Brut Imperial) predicted as STRONG SIGNAL.
Vintage Champagne (Pol Roger 2012) predicted as PLAUSIBLE.
Roederer Estate Anderson Valley predicted as PLAUSIBLE. Pelorus/Brut Quartet resolved. Chardonnay/Pinot Noir varieties match.

results.notes: Champagne NV + Champagne vintage + California traditional method predicted correctly. All within-method quality discrimination anticipated.

per_wine.notes: Amarone appassimento predicted as STRONG SIGNAL. Corvina/Corvinone/Rondinella resolved via appellation_varieties.json.
Brachetto d'Acqui predicted as PLAUSIBLE. 5.5% ABV Charmat/Asti-method sweet.

per_wine.actual_variety: Touriga Nacional/Touriga Franca/Tinta Roriz/Tinta Barroca/Tinto Cao
per_wine.notes: Vintage Port (Graham's 2000) predicted as STRONG SIGNAL. Port varieties resolved via appellation_varieties.json.
Maury VDN predicted as PLAUSIBLE. Grenache Noir resolved via appellation_varieties.json.
Rutherglen Muscat predicted as PLAUSIBLE. Stanton & Killeen named.

results.notes: All 5 mechanisms and origins predicted correctly. The five-mechanism sweep (appassimento, arrested fermentation, spirit addition, VDN, oxidative fortified blend) is the tree's most reliable P3 Q2 template.

results.question_structure: 4 wines in 2 pairs, each pair from different region (fortified)

per_wine.notes: Dry Oloroso from Jerez (Lustau Almacenista) predicted as STRONG SIGNAL. Palomino resolved via appellation_varieties.json.
Sweet Oloroso Dulce 30-year from Jerez (Gonzales Byass Matusalem) predicted as STRONG SIGNAL.
Entry-level Madeira (H&H Full Rich 3yo) predicted as STRONG SIGNAL. Tinta Negra is the default base grape for entry Madeira. Resolved via appellation_varieties.json.
Single Harvest Boal 2000 Madeira (H&H) predicted as STRONG SIGNAL. Boal is a noble Madeira variety. Same producer (Henriques & Henriques) for both Madeira wines predicted.

results.notes: Both pairs (Jerez dry vs sweet Oloroso, Madeira entry vs vintage) predicted exactly. All wines' styles, origins, and quality levels matched. Same producer in Madeira pair correctly anticipated.

variety_misses_detail.notes: Grillo was mentioned in candidate text as alternative Sicilian white but not as named top-1. Country and sub-region (Sicily) were correct.
Pinot Noir component predicted but blend with Merlot not anticipated. Scored as top-3 variety hit (rank 2) because primary component matched.

subregion_misses_detail.notes: Sub-region was actually correct (Sicily) despite variety miss. Recounted as hit.
Kim Crawford NZ-wide appellation. No sub-region mismatch.

summary.notes: Extremely high accuracy across both folds. The v2 decision matrices (Phase 5B tree-aware) demonstrate near-perfect prediction when the tree was built WITH these years in the training corpus. This is not a true out-of-sample test since the matrices were generated with knowledge of the actual wines (they contain reality check sections). The LOYO value lies in validating the scoring methodology and establishing a baseline, not in measuring generalizable predictive power. True LOYO would require trees built excluding these years.
